violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (food processor) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC placed in 3 bay sink to be washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2025

Various items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. ( ) was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview, potatoes was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC placed in shallow container. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2025
Cooking oil was observed stored on the floor in the back storage room. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -prep units, -prep table shelf, -spice grinder, -blender base, -meat grinder. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2025

Prep sink was lacking an air gap. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2025

Raw animal foods (such as meats and eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as produce and cooked foods) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC reorganized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025

Poultry was cooked to 157 degrees Fahrenheit. Poultry must be cooked to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. PIC reheated to 172 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025

Garlic was observed held at 52 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025

Various food items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025
Per interview, PIC stated the establishment par cooks chicken and fish. PIC informed a variance is required. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview with PIC, ribs were placed hot in individually covered containers and stacked on top of each other in the 2 door lowboy. Rice mixed with chicken was cooling on a counter in a pan greater than four inches deep. PIC placed both products in shallower pans and put into walk-in. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025

Raw, frozen meat was observed thawing on a speed rack in the kitchen area. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: -under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); -completely submerged under running water; -as part of the cooking process; or -in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. PIC placed in walk-in cooler. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025

Various food items were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen area, walk-in cooler, and storage area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC corrected. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025
Food and non-food was observed stored together in the dry storage area. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025

Yogurt sauce was observed in container previously used to stored chemicals and samosas were observed stored in a cardboard box previously used to store potatoes. PIC disposed and informed to cease practice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. PIC removed bowls. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:-prep units,-deep freezer,-prep tables,-spice grinder,-blender bases,-meat grinder,-mixer,-storage shelves. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025

The prep sink is lacking an air gap. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2025
The prep sink was observed in poor repair (clogged pipe). New Violation. Correct By: 04/12/2025

The dumpster outside is located on dirt. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2025
Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025
The ceiling in the kitchen area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/17/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (meat skewers) were observed with rust. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch.PIC to wash and sanitize before using. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2024
The (walls) in the (back storage ) area is in poor repair. Observed a hole. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2024
The ceiling in the (kitchen) area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/17/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (meat grinder) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2024

The cutting boards in the (kitchen and/or on prep units ) are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2024

The following non-food contact surfaces observed with an accumulation debris: 1.all refrigerator units 2.all chest freezers 3.prep tables and shelves 4.floor mixer 5.meat grinder cart New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2024

The walls in the (storage room behind chest freezer ) are constructed of (dry wall). This material is not durable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2024
The (walls ) in the (back storage area ) area is in poor repair. Observed holes. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2024

The floors and walls in the walk in cooler and kitchen area have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2024
